features.description: Packaged, reusable abilities the agent can invoke

features.name: Agent tool integration manifest
features.description: Machine-readable declaration of the agent's tool surface (which CLIs/APIs/MCP servers the agent is allowed to use). PASS via any of: (a) MCP manifest with at least one server entry; (b) `.claude/settings.json` with non-empty `permissions.allow` or `permissions.deny`; (c) `.factory/settings.json` with `commandAllowlist`/`commandDenylist`; (d) Codex agent-runner config; (e) repo-local agent-tool-policy doc cross-referenced from AGENTS.md; (f) **explicit opt-out**: a manifest file (`.claude/settings.json`, `.mcp.json`, etc.) that contains a `"policy": "intentionally-unscoped"` field OR a `"_comment"` explaining the choice (e.g., `"_comment": "Solo private repo, agent has unrestricted local access by design"`). Mark `—` (N/A) when `team_scale=solo AND visibility=private AND no manifest file exists at all` (the criterion is structurally premature for a single-developer prototype). FAIL only when: a manifest file exists with truly empty/default scoping AND there is no explicit opt-out AND the repo is not solo-private. Stub configs without rationale on multi-contributor or public repos still fail -- that's where the agent's tool surface ambiguity actually causes problems.
features.evidence: MCP manifest, `.claude/settings.json` with non-empty `permissions`, `.factory/settings.json` with `commandAllowlist`, agent-tool-policy doc, OR explicit `"policy": "intentionally-unscoped"` / `"_comment"` rationale in the manifest

features.name: Runnable examples
features.description: Working example code the agent can study and imitate. **Libraries / SDKs / CLIs / frameworks**: strict -- require `examples/`, `_examples/`, or example apps with READMEs that exercise the public API. **Apps**: broaden -- accept tested recipes (`docs/recipes/`), pattern docs (`docs/patterns/`), playbooks (`docs/playbooks/`), or curated route/component reference implementations that are explicitly referenced from AGENTS.md/README as imitation targets. Tests alone do NOT pass; empty/unreferenced directories do NOT pass. **Monorepos**: apply per-package (strict for published packages, app rule for contained apps).
features.evidence: Libraries: `examples/`, `_examples/`, example apps with READMEs. Apps: `docs/recipes/`, `docs/patterns/`, `docs/playbooks/` cross-linked from AGENTS.md/README

features.name: PR template
features.description: Template that guides PR descriptions toward reviewable AND recoverable changes. File existence is necessary but NOT sufficient. PASS requires the template to include at minimum: (a) a **Summary** or **Description** section; (b) a **Testing** section -- either a checklist with concrete commands the reviewer can verify, OR a free-form section that names what was tested; (c) a **Risk / Rollback** section (or equivalent: "How to revert", "Blast radius", "Recovery plan") -- this is the recoverability signal Pillar 7 cares about, and the criterion now enforces it even for L2. Optional but encouraged: Breaking Changes section, Related Issues / Closes, Checklist (secrets, conventional commits, scope). FAIL when the file exists but is missing any of the three required sections, OR when it contains only a `## Summary` heading with no other guidance. See the recommended template in `docs/factory-ai-readiness/remediate-prompts/2-taskdiscovery-pr-templates.md`.
features.evidence: `.github/pull_request_template.md` (or `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`, or GitLab `.gitlab/merge_request_templates/`) containing Summary + Testing + Risk/Rollback sections

features.name: License
features.description: The agent unambiguously knows the licensing posture. Accepts any of: (a) a LICENSE/MIT-LICENSE/COPYING/LICENSE.md file; (b) a clear proprietary-copyright statement at the top of README ("All Rights Reserved", "Proprietary - © {Company}") combined with a `package.json` signal (`"license": "UNLICENSED"` or `"private": true`); (c) a dedicated COPYRIGHT file with a proprietary notice. Vague statements ("This is a private project") do NOT pass. **For published OSS packages** (`package.json` has `publishConfig` or `"private": false`), require an SPDX-compliant LICENSE file -- README mention alone is insufficient.
features.evidence: `LICENSE`, `MIT-LICENSE`, `COPYING`, `LICENSE.md`, `COPYRIGHT`, OR README "All Rights Reserved" + package.json proprietary signal

features.name: Governance model

Evidence varies by repo profile: **public OSS / external-contributor repos** require formal `GOVERNANCE.md` / `MAINTAINERS.md` with maintainer roles + decision process. **Internal multi-team repos** can satisfy via `CODEOWNERS` + escalation docs (e.g., `docs/operations/escalation.md`, team-ownership table in AGENTS.md, on-call rotation doc). Mark `—` (N/A) only when `team_scale=solo AND visibility=private`.

features.name: DAST scanning
features.description: Dynamic security testing in CI
features.evidence: OWASP ZAP, Burp, Nuclei, Probely workflow on staging. Mark `—` (N/A) for small static/SSG sites, marketing pages, or directory listings with no user input beyond contact forms -- the cost of standing up a DAST pipeline outweighs the threat. Apps with auth, multi-tenant data, payment flows, or APIs accepting untrusted input should still fail this if no DAST is configured.
features.conditional_note: conditional: web-facing app with meaningful attack surface

features.name: Cross-platform support
features.description: **Libraries / SDKs / CLIs / frameworks**: strict -- require multi-OS CI matrix, cross-compilation, or multi-arch Docker. A library failing to test on macOS/Windows ships brittle artifacts. **Apps (hosted)**: evaluate dev-loop portability instead of artifact portability. PASS when local task runners (npm scripts, Makefile, Justfile) normalize paths/configs across darwin/linux/win -- e.g., `cross-env` usage, no hard-coded `/bin/sh`-only or `cmd.exe`-only invocations, no unnormalized path separators, or a documented "Windows users: use WSL" policy with WSL setup steps. FAIL when scripts demonstrably break on a major dev platform with no documented workaround. The deploy target (Vercel Linux, Fly, Render, etc.) being single-OS is NOT itself a failure for apps. **Monorepos**: apply per-package.
features.evidence: Libraries: CI matrix with multiple OS, cross-compilation, multi-arch Docker. Apps: cross-env usage, portable scripts, OR documented single-OS-dev policy with WSL/devcontainer escape hatch

features.name: Cloud dev environment
features.description: Cloud-based workspace configuration
features.evidence: `.devcontainer/` with Codespaces features, `.gitpod.yml`, GitHub Codespaces ready

features.name: Package manager determinism
features.description: The agent can reproduce the same dependency tree across machines. Accepts any of: `.npmrc`/`.yarnrc.yml`/`.pnpmfile.cjs` with `engine-strict` / frozen-lockfile / registry pinning; `"packageManager"` field in `package.json` (Corepack pin); `"engines"` enforced in CI (e.g., `npm ci --engine-strict`); `.tool-versions` / `mise.toml` / `.node-version` runtime pin; CI workflow uses `npm ci` / `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` / `yarn install --immutable`; equivalents for `pip.conf`, `.cargo/config.toml`. Lockfile alone is NOT sufficient (already covered by #91). Fails only when installation is genuinely ambiguous: no lockfile pin + no runtime pin + no CI-frozen-install discipline.
features.evidence: `.npmrc`, `.yarnrc.yml`, `"packageManager"` field, Corepack, `engines` enforced in CI, `.tool-versions`, `mise.toml`, `pip.conf`, `.cargo/config.toml`

features.name: Distributed tracing
features.description: Request tracing within or across services
features.evidence: OTel tracing SDK, Jaeger/Tempo backend, trace propagation middleware. Single-service apps PASS when an APM/tracing SDK is wired (e.g. `@sentry/nextjs` with `instrumentation.ts` / `instrumentation-client.ts`, or `@opentelemetry/*` deps with an exporter configured) -- per-request trace IDs and breadcrumb context still produce the diagnostic value the criterion measures.
features.conditional_note: conditional: any production system that emits requests, single-service included

features.name: Alerting configured
features.description: Paging integration, alert rules, OR an error->issue pipeline that routes signals to humans
features.evidence: Alert rules in CI/CD, PagerDuty/OpsGenie/Slack integration, on-call rotation. Also passes when an automated pipeline turns errors into actionable work items -- e.g. a `.github/workflows/sentry-issue.yml` + `src/app/api/sentry-webhook/route.ts` that creates a labeled GitHub issue per Sentry alert. The bar is "someone notices and can act", not "PagerDuty specifically".

features.evidence: SonarCloud, Codacy, Code Climate, custom dashboards

features.name: Profiling instrumentation
features.description: Continuous or on-demand profiling
features.evidence: py-spy, pprof, Node `--prof`, async-profiler, Datadog continuous profiler

features.name: Circuit breakers
features.description: Resilience pattern around remote calls
features.evidence: Hystrix, Resilience4j, opossum, polly, custom retry/timeout middleware

features.name: Error → insight pipeline
features.description: Errors flow from tracking to actionable issues
features.evidence: Sentry-GitHub integration, webhook → issue/ticket, error-grouping → PR draft

features.name: Tool allowlist / permission policy
features.description: Documented or enforced list of CLIs/APIs/destinations the agent may use
features.evidence: `.claude/settings.json` `permissions` block, `.factory/settings.json` `commandAllowlist`/`commandDenylist`, agent-tool allowlist file (note: `.factory/policy.json` does NOT exist — Factory's real path is `settings.json`)

features.name: Sandboxing / blast-radius bounds
features.description: Agent runs in a constrained environment with limited filesystem/network access
features.evidence: Devcontainer with restricted mounts, agent-runner sandbox script, network egress allowlist

features.name: Hooks for context preservation
features.description: Pre/post-action hooks that capture or persist agent state
features.evidence: `.claude/hooks/`, `.factory/hooks/`, PreCompact / SessionEnd / PreCommit agent hooks

features.name: SBOM presence
features.description: Software Bill of Materials generated or committed
features.evidence: `sbom.json`, `cyclonedx.json`, syft/grype output, SBOM upload in release workflow

features.name: Provenance attestations
features.description: Cryptographic attestations on builds/commits
features.evidence: `sigstore` / `cosign` signing, in-toto attestations, GitHub `actions/attest-build-provenance`

features.name: Agent audit trail with stable run IDs
features.description: Every agent action is logged with a run ID linkable to commit/PR/deploy
features.evidence: Run-ID convention in commit messages or PR labels, agent-run log dir, telemetry hooks

features.name: Replayable evaluation harness
features.description: Agent behavior tested against fixed scenarios
features.evidence: `evals/` directory, golden-prompt fixtures, agent regression-test workflow in CI

features.name: Kill-switch infrastructure
features.description: Mechanism to halt agent activity quickly
features.evidence: Feature flag scoped to "agent runs," disable-agent script, repo-level circuit breaker

features.name: Human escalation path
features.description: Documented handoff with sufficient context for a human to take over
features.evidence: Agent leaves a status note, `escalate-to-human.md` template, PR description includes context

features.name: Agent registry / ownership metadata
features.description: Documented map of which agent owns which scope
features.evidence: `.agents/registry.yaml`, agent-ownership table in AGENTS.md, agent identity in CODEOWNERS

features.name: Per-repo policy visibility
features.description: Agent can read its constraints (off-limits paths, approval-required actions)
features.evidence: Machine-readable policy file (`agent-policy.json`, `.agent/restricted-paths.md`), referenced from AGENTS.md

features.name: Cost telemetry for agent runs
features.description: Token usage / run cost is tracked and attributable
features.evidence: OTel attributes for agent ops, agent-cost log line per run, cost-per-PR dashboard
